cards.back: The level assigned to each operator that determines which operators are grouped with operands first. Operators with higher precedence are evaluated before operators with lower precedence.

cards.text: In the expression 4 + 2 * 3, multiplication has {{c1::higher}} precedence than addition, so it evaluates as {{c2::4 + (2 * 3)}} = {{c3::10}}

cards.back: When two operators with the same precedence level are adjacent, associativity determines whether to evaluate from left to right or right to left.

cards.question: How is this expression grouped?
cards.explanation: Subtraction has left-to-right associativity, so it evaluates from left to right as (7 - 4) - 1 = 3 - 1 = 2

cards.text: Multiplication and division have precedence level {{c1::5}}, while addition and subtraction have precedence level {{c2::6}}. {{c3::Lower}} numbers mean higher precedence.

cards.front: Why should you use parentheses even when not strictly required?
cards.back: To disambiguate operator precedence and make code more readable. It removes any question or opportunity for confusion about the order of operations.

cards.text: The global scope operator is {{c1::::}}, and the class scope operator is {{c2::class_name::member_name}}

cards.front: Which has higher precedence: assignment (=) or arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /)?
cards.back: Arithmetic operators have higher precedence. Assignment has one of the lowest precedence levels (precedence level 16).

cards.question: What value does x get assigned?
cards.explanation: Multiplication has higher precedence than addition, so it evaluates as 5 + (2 * 3) = 5 + 6 = 11

cards.concept: When to use parentheses with operators
cards.explanation: Always use parentheses to disambiguate the precedence of operators if there is any question or opportunity for confusion. This makes code more readable and prevents bugs.

cards.text: Operator precedence determines {{c1::which operators are grouped with operands first}}, while associativity determines {{c2::whether to evaluate left-to-right or right-to-left}}.
Precedence level {{c1::1}} is the {{c2::highest}} precedence, while level 17 is the {{c3::lowest}}.
